seoTitle: Aishi Aluminum Electrolytic Capacitors Product Catalog | BeiLuo Electronics
seoDescription: Browse Aishi aluminum electrolytic capacitors, radial lead, snap-in, screw terminal, and solid polymer capacitors. Technical specifications, selection guide, and alternative parts.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Aishi capacitor for my application?
faqs.answer: Consider voltage rating (1.2-1.5x operating voltage), capacitance value, ripple current requirements, temperature range, physical size, and lifetime requirements. Aishi offers comprehensive selection guides for each product series.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team with your application specifications for personalized Aishi capacitor selection assistance.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Aishi's radial and snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Radial capacitors have wire leads for through-hole mounting and are suitable for lower power applications. Snap-in capacitors have PCB mounting terminals, higher capacitance values, and are designed for high-power applications like SMPS and LED drivers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use radial capacitors for consumer electronics and low-power applications. Use snap-in capacitors for power supplies above 100W and industrial applications.

faqs.question: What are Aishi's screw terminal capacitors used for?
faqs.answer: Screw terminal capacitors are designed for high-power applications requiring large capacitance values and high ripple current capability. Common applications include inverters, welding equipment, UPS systems, and industrial motor drives.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications above 500W or requiring capacitance above 10,000uF, consider screw terminal capacitors for reliable connections.

faqs.question: Are Aishi capacitors RoHS compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, all Aishi capacitors are RoHS compliant and free from lead, mercury, cadmium, and other restricted substances. Aishi also provides halogen-free options for environmentally sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For RoHS compliance documentation, contact our sales team with your specific part numbers.

faqs.question: What is the lead time for Aishi capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard Aishi capacitors typically have 4-6 weeks lead time. High-volume or custom specifications may require 8-10 weeks. BeiLuo Electronics maintains inventory of popular Aishi models for faster delivery.
faqs.decisionGuide: For urgent requirements, check our stock availability or discuss scheduled orders with our sales team.

categories.name: Radial Lead Capacitors
categories.description: Standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitors for general-purpose applications
categories.longDescription: Aishi radial lead capacitors are the industry standard for general-purpose aluminum electrolytic capacitors. These capacitors feature wire leads for through-hole mounting and are available in a wide range of capacitance and voltage ratings. Ideal for consumer electronics, power supplies, and industrial applications where compact size and cost-effectiveness are important. Contact BeiLuo Electronics, your authorized Aishi distributor, for radial capacitor selection guidance.
categories.selectionGuide: Select based on capacitance, voltage rating, size constraints, and lifetime requirements. RS series for standard, RH for high temperature, RT for low impedance.

faqs.question: What are the typical applications for Aishi radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Radial capacitors are used in consumer electronics, power supplies, audio equipment, LED drivers, and industrial controls. They are ideal for applications requiring compact size and cost-effective solutions.
faqs.decisionGuide: For general-purpose applications with moderate capacitance requirements, radial capacitors offer the best cost-performance ratio.

faqs.question: What is the difference between RS, RH, and RT series?
faqs.answer: RS series is the standard radial capacitor for general applications. RH series offers higher temperature ratings (up to 125C) and longer lifetime. RT series features low impedance for high-frequency applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use RS series for standard applications up to 85C. Use RH series for high-temperature applications. Use RT series for switching power supplies.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Aishi radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard radial capacitors are rated for 2,000 to 10,000 hours at 85C or 105C. Lifetime can be extended significantly with proper derating and lower operating temperatures following the Arrhenius equation.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, operate at lower temperatures and use voltage derating. Calculate expected lifetime using the Arrhenius equation.

faqs.question: Can Aishi radial capacitors be used in LED driver applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aishi radial capacitors are widely used in LED drivers, particularly for output filtering. Select capacitors with appropriate lifetime ratings and ripple current capability for your LED application.
faqs.decisionGuide: For LED drivers, select capacitors with 8,000+ hour lifetime ratings and sufficient ripple current capability. Consider RH series for high-temperature environments.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current rating of Aishi radial capacitors?
faqs.answer: Ripple current ratings for Aishi radial capacitors range from 0.1A to 2.5A depending on capacitance, voltage rating, and case size. Higher capacitance values and larger case sizes typically offer higher ripple current capability. The ripple current rating is specified at 100-120Hz and must be derated for higher frequencies. Temperature also affects ripple current capability - ratings are specified at 85°C or 105°C, and capability increases at lower temperatures. Always select capacitors with ripple current ratings at least 1.5x your application's calculated ripple current to ensure reliable operation and long lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacitors with ripple current ratings at least 1.5x your application requirements. Consider frequency and temperature derating for accurate selection.

faqs.question: Are Aishi radial capacitors available in low-profile versions?
faqs.answer: Yes, Aishi offers low-profile radial capacitors with reduced height specifically designed for space-constrained applications. These low-profile versions maintain the same electrical performance as standard capacitors while offering reduced diameter-to-height ratios. They are ideal for slim LED drivers, compact power supplies, and applications with height restrictions. The low-profile design allows for more compact product designs without sacrificing reliability or performance. Available in various capacitance and voltage ratings to meet different application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: For height-restricted applications, specify low-profile versions with reduced diameter-to-height ratios while maintaining required electrical specifications.

products.name: Radial Capacitor 1000uF 25V
products.shortDescription: Standard radial capacitor with 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating for general-purpose applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The RS-1000uF-25V is a standard radial aluminum electrolytic capacitor designed for general-purpose applications. With 1000uF capacitance and 25V rating, it provides reliable filtering and decoupling for low-voltage circuits.
This capacitor features a compact 10mm diameter case with 5mm lead spacing, making it ideal for space-constrained designs. The standard radial form factor enables easy through-hole mounting on PCBs.

Rated for 2000 hours at 85C, this capacitor is suitable for consumer electronics and industrial applications with standard lifetime requirements. The cost-effective design makes it ideal for high-volume production.

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE
faeReview.content: The RS-1000uF-25V is my standard recommendation for low-voltage filtering applications. The 1000uF/25V rating is perfect for 12V and 5V power supply output filtering. The compact 10mm size fits in most designs, and the 5mm lead spacing is compatible with standard PCB layouts. For consumer electronics where cost is critical, this capacitor offers excellent value. I recommend keeping the operating voltage below 20V for extended lifetime. In my experience, this capacitor provides reliable performance in LED drivers, consumer electronics, and industrial controls. The 2000 hour lifetime at 85C is adequate for most applications, and proper derating can extend this significantly.
faeReview.highlight: Standard for low-voltage filtering; perfect for 12V/5V supplies; compact 10mm size; excellent value

alternativeParts.reason: Higher temperature rating for demanding environments

applicationScenarios.description: Output filtering for 5V DC power supply

faqs.question: What is the maximum ripple current for the RS-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: The rated ripple current is 1.2A at 85C and 120Hz. This rating ensures reliable operation in power supply applications with moderate switching frequencies. At lower temperatures, the capacitor can handle higher ripple currents - approximately 1.4A at 65C. For high-frequency applications above 100kHz, the effective ripple current capability may be reduced due to increased ESR. Always verify the actual operating temperature and frequency conditions in your application to ensure the capacitor operates within its safe operating area.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications exceeding 1.2A ripple current, consider using multiple capacitors in parallel or upgrading to a higher capacitance model. Contact our FAE team for thermal modeling and ripple current calculations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime of the RS-1000uF-25V at typical operating conditions?
faqs.answer: The rated lifetime is 2,000 hours at 85C. Using the Arrhenius equation, we can calculate the expected lifetime at different operating temperatures. At 65C, the expected lifetime extends to 8,000 hours. At 45C, lifetime increases to 32,000 hours. At 25C ambient, you can achieve up to 128,000 hours. Every 10C reduction in operating temperature approximately doubles the capacitor lifetime. These calculations assume voltage derating to 80% of rated voltage and ripple current within specifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: For maximum capacitor lifetime, operate at the lowest practical temperature with 80% voltage derating. Contact our FAE team for lifetime calculations specific to your application conditions.

faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended for reliable operation of the RS-1000uF-25V?
faqs.answer: Industry best practice recommends operating aluminum electrolytic capacitors at no more than 80% of their rated voltage. For this 25V rated capacitor, the maximum recommended operating voltage is 20V. This 20% derating significantly improves reliability and extends operational lifetime. For critical applications or high-temperature environments, 50% derating is recommended, limiting operating voltage to 13V. The voltage derating also provides margin for voltage transients and line regulation variations. Operating above 80% of rated voltage will significantly reduce capacitor lifetime.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design your circuit to operate the capacitor at 80% or less of rated voltage for optimal reliability. Contact our FAE team for voltage derating recommendations specific to your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the operating temperature range of the RS-1000uF-25V and how does it affect performance?
faqs.answer: This capacitor is rated for operation from -40C to +85C. At the maximum rated temperature of 85C, the capacitor achieves its rated lifetime of 2,000 hours. As operating temperature decreases, capacitor lifetime increases exponentially following the Arrhenius relationship - approximately doubling for every 10C reduction. Capacitance and ESR also vary with temperature, with capacitance typically increasing 10-15% at low temperatures and decreasing slightly at high temperatures. ESR generally decreases as temperature increases, improving high-frequency performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended capacitor lifetime, minimize operating temperature through proper thermal design and ventilation. Contact our FAE team for thermal management recommendations specific to your application.

faqs.question: What is the ESR of the RS-1000uF-25V and how does it affect circuit performance?
faqs.answer: The Equivalent Series Resistance (ESR) at 100Hz is approximately 0.02 Ohm. ESR represents the resistive losses in the capacitor and directly affects ripple voltage, power dissipation, and filtering effectiveness. Lower ESR results in lower ripple voltage for a given ripple current, reduced self-heating, and improved filtering at high frequencies. This capacitor's ESR characteristics make it suitable for switching power supplies and industrial controls. ESR typically decreases with increasing temperature and increases at higher frequencies. For switching power supplies operating at 100kHz, consider the frequency characteristics of ESR.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lower ESR, consider our low-impedance series or connect multiple capacitors in parallel. Contact our FAE team for ESR optimization recommendations.

faqs.question: Can multiple RS-1000uF-25V capacitors be connected in parallel for increased capability?
faqs.answer: Yes, multiple capacitors can be connected in parallel to increase total capacitance and share ripple current. When connecting RS-1000uF-25V in parallel, the total capacitance adds linearly while ESR reduces proportionally. For example, two capacitors in parallel provide 2000uF capacitance and approximately 0.010 Ohm ESR (half the single capacitor ESR). This configuration also improves ripple current capability to 2.4A. Important considerations include: using identical part numbers for balanced current sharing, maintaining symmetrical PCB layout, ensuring adequate spacing for thermal management, and verifying the total inrush current doesn't exceed circuit limitations.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-current applications, consider parallel connection of multiple capacitors. Contact our FAE team for parallel capacitor bank design assistance and current sharing analysis.

faqs.question: What are snap-in capacitors used for?
faqs.answer: Snap-in capacitors are designed for high-power applications requiring large capacitance values (1,000uF to 100,000uF) and high ripple current capability. Common applications include switch-mode power supplies, LED drivers, inverters, welding equipment, and industrial motor drives. The snap-in terminals provide secure PCB mounting for high-vibration environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use snap-in capacitors for power supplies above 100W, applications requiring capacitance above 1,000uF, and high-vibration industrial environments.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SN, SH, and SL series?
faqs.answer: SN series is the standard snap-in capacitor for general power supply applications. SH series offers higher temperature ratings (up to 105C) for demanding thermal environments. SL series provides extended lifetime ratings (up to 20,000 hours) for maintenance-free operation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SN series for standard power supplies. Use SH series for high-temperature applications. Use SL series for long-lifetime requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lifetime of Aishi snap-in capacitors?
faqs.answer: Standard snap-in capacitors are rated for 2,000 to 20,000 hours depending on series and temperature rating. SN series: 2,000-5,000 hours at 85C. SH series: 5,000-10,000 hours at 105C. SL series: 10,000-20,000 hours at 105C. Lifetime can be extended significantly with proper derating.
faqs.decisionGuide: For extended lifetime, select SL series and operate at lower temperatures with voltage derating.

faqs.question: What ripple current can snap-in capacitors handle?
faqs.answer: Snap-in capacitors offer high ripple current capability ranging from 2A to 20A depending on capacitance, voltage, and case size. Larger cases and higher capacitance values provide higher ripple current ratings. The ripple current capability is essential for handling the switching currents in power supply applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select capacitors with ripple current ratings at least 1.5x your application requirements. Contact our FAE team for ripple current calculations.

faqs.question: Are snap-in capacitors suitable for LED driver applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, snap-in capacitors are widely used in high-power LED drivers (100W+) for input filtering, PFC output, and DC output stages. Their high capacitance values and ripple current capability make them ideal for LED applications requiring long lifetime and high reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: For LED drivers above 100W, select snap-in capacitors with 8,000+ hour lifetime ratings. Consider SL series for premium LED fixtures.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime?
faqs.answer: Rated lifetime is 5,000 hours at 85°C. Using the Arrhenius equation, lifetime extends to approximately 20,000 hours at 65°C and 80,000 hours at 45°C. For applications requiring longer lifetime, consider the SH series with 105°C rating and 8,000+ hour lifetime. faqs.question: What voltage derating is recommended?

Operating at 80% voltage can provide 2-4x longer life compared to operating at 100% rated voltage. For critical applications, 50% derating may be used for maximum reliability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Design for 80% voltage derating minimum. Use 50% derating for critical applications where maximum reliability is required.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of solid polymer capacitors?
faqs.answer: Solid polymer capacitors offer ultra-low ESR (10x lower than electrolytic), much higher ripple current capability, longer lifetime (no drying out), stable capacitance over temperature, and no risk of leakage. They are ideal for high-frequency applications and where reliability is critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use solid polymer capacitors for high-frequency switching supplies, high-reliability applications, and where low ESR is critical.

faqs.question: What is the typical ESR of solid polymer capacitors?
faqs.answer: Solid polymer capacitors offer ESR values 10-50 times lower than equivalent electrolytic capacitors. Typical ESR ranges from 5mOhm to 50mOhm depending on capacitance and voltage rating. This ultra-low ESR enables much higher ripple current capability and better filtering at high frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring lowest ESR, select solid polymer capacitors over electrolytic.

faqs.question: What is the lifetime of solid polymer capacitors?
faqs.answer: Solid polymer capacitors offer extremely long lifetime ratings from 10,000 to 100,000+ hours. Unlike electrolytic capacitors, they do not dry out over time since they use solid polymer instead of liquid electrolyte. This makes them ideal for applications requiring maintenance-free operation over many years.
faqs.decisionGuide: For applications requiring 10+ year lifetime, solid polymer capacitors are the best choice.

faqs.question: Are solid polymer capacitors more expensive?
faqs.answer: Yes, solid polymer capacitors typically cost 3-5x more than equivalent electrolytic capacitors. However, the superior performance, longer lifetime, and higher reliability often justify the cost in critical applications. The total cost of ownership may be lower when considering replacement and maintenance costs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider total cost of ownership including reliability and maintenance when selecting solid polymer capacitors.

faqs.question: What applications benefit most from solid polymer capacitors?
faqs.answer: Applications benefiting most include high-frequency switching power supplies (100kHz+), computer motherboards and graphics cards, server power supplies, telecom equipment, automotive electronics, and any application where low ESR, high ripple current, and long lifetime are critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use solid polymer for high-frequency, high-reliability, and critical applications.

faqs.question: What makes solid polymer capacitors different from electrolytic?
faqs.answer: Solid polymer capacitors use a conductive solid polymer instead of liquid electrolyte. This provides ultra-low ESR (5-50mOhm vs 50-500mOhm for electrolytic), much higher ripple current capability, longer lifetime (no drying out), stable performance over temperature, and no risk of leakage. The trade-off is higher cost (3-5x) and typically lower maximum voltage ratings.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use solid polymer when low ESR, high ripple current, and long lifetime are critical. Use electrolytic for cost-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: What is the ESR and why is it important?
faqs.answer: ESR (Equivalent Series Resistance) is the resistive component of a capacitor's impedance. This capacitor has just 8mOhm ESR - about 20x lower than an equivalent electrolytic capacitor. Low ESR is critical because it reduces ripple voltage, minimizes power dissipation (heat), and improves filtering effectiveness at high frequencies. In VRM applications, low ESR directly translates to better voltage regulation and lower ripple.
faqs.decisionGuide: For high-frequency applications, always select capacitors with lowest practical ESR.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current capability?
faqs.answer: Rated ripple current is 8A at 105C and 100kHz. This exceptional ripple current capability is enabled by the ultra-low ESR. The capacitor can handle the high switching currents typical of modern DC-DC converters without excessive heating. At lower temperatures, even higher ripple currents can be supported.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 8A rating is suitable for high-current VRM and DC-DC applications.

faqs.question: How long will this capacitor last?

faqs.question: What is the ESR of this capacitor?
faqs.answer: The ESR (Equivalent Series Resistance) is 15mOhm maximum at 100kHz. This is approximately 10-15x lower than an equivalent aluminum electrolytic capacitor. The ultra-low ESR provides excellent filtering at high frequencies and minimizes power dissipation and heat generation. Low ESR is critical for high-frequency switching applications as it directly affects voltage ripple and power efficiency. In DC-DC converter applications, the low ESR helps maintain tight voltage regulation during load transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: The 15mOhm ESR is excellent for high-frequency filtering applications. Select solid polymer capacitors when lowest ESR is required.

faqs.question: What is the ripple current rating?
faqs.answer: Rated ripple current is 4A at 105°C and 100kHz. This high ripple current capability is enabled by the ultra-low ESR of 15mOhm. The capacitor can handle the high switching currents typical of DC-DC converters without excessive heating. At lower temperatures, even higher ripple currents can be supported - approximately 5A at 85°C. This makes the capacitor suitable for input filtering in DC-DC converters and distributed power systems where high ripple currents are present.
faqs.decisionGuide: 4A rating is suitable for most DC-DC converter input filtering applications. Consider temperature derating for accurate selection.

faqs.question: What is the expected lifetime?
faqs.answer: Rated lifetime is 100,000 hours at 105C. At typical operating temperatures of 60-80C, expected lifetime extends to 200,000-400,000+ hours. Solid polymer capacitors don't dry out, maintaining performance over time.
faqs.decisionGuide: Essentially maintenance-free for most applications.

faqs.question: What applications is this suitable for?

faqs.decisionGuide: Perfect for input filtering in 12V/24V systems.

faqs.question: How does this compare to electrolytic capacitors?
faqs.answer: Compared to electrolytic: ESR is 10-15x lower (15mOhm vs 150-250mOhm), ripple current is 5-10x higher (4A vs 0.5-1A), lifetime is 50x longer (100,000h vs 2,000h), but cost is 3-5x higher. The performance advantages often justify the cost in critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose solid polymer when performance and reliability are critical; choose electrolytic for cost-sensitive applications.
